paper_title: Sulphate partitioning into calcite: Experimental verification of pH control and application to seasonality in speleothems

methods.description: This method analyzes the mechanisms of sulphate incorporation into calcite, focusing on the effects of pH, saturation state, and defect sites on the partition coefficient (D SO4). It aims to understand how these factors influence sulphate content in calcite, which is crucial for interpreting speleothem records.

steps.description: Determine the relationship between sulphate content in calcite and pH at different aqueous sulphate concentrations.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sulphate content in calcite as a function of pH
outputs: relationship between sulphate content and pH

steps.description: Evaluate the effect of growth rate on D SO4 at constant pH.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze effect of growth rate on partition coefficient at constant pH
outputs: D SO4 values at different growth rates

steps.description: Assess the impact of bicarbonate competition on sulphate incorporation into calcite.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate bicarbonate competition effect on sulphate incorporation
outputs: impact of bicarbonate on sulphate incorporation

steps.description: Investigate the role of defect sites in promoting sulphate incorporation at higher supersaturation states.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze defect site influence on sulphate incorporation
outputs: relationship between defect sites and sulphate incorporation
